commit.message:
Fix PPO date picker click: use elementFromPoint + pointer events (Order 367 method)

The only successful date picker test (Order 367) used elementFromPoint to find
the actual child element at click coordinates, then dispatched pointer events on
it. React Native Web's Responder system responds to events that bubble UP from
child elements (span, SVG path), not from parent containers. Previous approach
dispatched directly on the container, which didn't trigger the Responder.

Now tries 3 methods in sequence:
1. CDP mouse.click (most realistic browser simulation)
2. elementFromPoint + pointer events (Order 367's proven approach)
3. Right-side offset click (targeting SVG chevron area)

Also adds diagnostic logging when calendar fails to appear.

commit.message:
feat: add boot recovery, power hardening, and OOBE suppression

Addresses two BSOD incidents (0x1C7 STORE_DATA_STRUCTURE_CORRUPTION and
0x133 DPC_WATCHDOG_VIOLATION) on fanless kiosk hardware where idle
power-state transitions caused driver instability.

- BootRecoveryHelper (new): bcdedit sets bootstatuspolicy to
  ignoreallfailures and recoveryenabled no so the machine auto-reboots
  after a BSOD instead of stopping on the Windows recovery screen
- PowerSettingsHelper: activates High Performance plan (SCHEME_MIN) and
  disables USB Selective Suspend to keep CPU/USB out of idle low-power states
- NotificationHelper: suppresses Windows OOBE / "Set up your PC" dialog
  that re-triggers after recovery boots (4 registry keys, HKLM + HKCU)
- MainForm: adds Boot Recovery checkbox to SysConfig panel; fixes panel
  overflow so all 6 items fit within ContentHeight=290; updates Summary
  panel for 11 rows; expands Verify panel to 10 rows
- Settings: adds BootRecoveryEnabled property
- VerifyHelper: replaces screensaver registry check with powercfg
  active-scheme check to confirm SCHEME_MIN is active
- README: documents new Boot Recovery feature, updated Display & Sleep
  and Notifications descriptions, adds Security Notes entry
- .gitignore: excludes .claude/ session data directory

commit.message:
Background build for synced MP3 with polling

Large recordings (840+ clips) timed out when built synchronously.
Now the audio endpoint works in three phases:
1. If synced.mp3 exists in storage → 302 redirect (instant)
2. If build is in progress → 202 with status JSON
3. Otherwise → starts background build task, returns 202

Client polls every 5 seconds until the file is ready, with a
persistent progress toast. Duplicate clicks are ignored.

commit.message:
fix(ci): hourly reviewer prefers PRs over issues for small fixes (#1432)

## Summary

- Rewrite the hourly reviewer's Step 7 ("Report findings") to prefer
creating PRs over issues
- PRs are now the default for any fixable problem (skill edits, workflow
tweaks, CLAUDE.md updates)
- Issues are only for problems too large or ambiguous to fix directly
- PR descriptions carry the full analysis (run ID, log excerpts, root
cause) — replacing what would have been the issue body
- Instructs the bot to keep findings cohesive (grouped PRs, not many
small patches)

This eliminates the duplication problem from #1425/#1426, where the
hourly reviewer created an issue and the triage bot raced to also fix
it. With the reviewer going straight to PR, there's no issue for triage
to pick up.

Requested by @max-sixty in
https://github.com/max-sixty/worktrunk/pull/1425#issuecomment-4034106262.

## Test plan

- [ ] Workflow YAML passes validation (CI lint check)
- [ ] Next hourly run creates PRs instead of issues for small fixes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: worktrunk-bot <254187624+worktrunk-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Maximilian Roos <5635139+max-sixty@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
improve: upgrade AgentPropsMatchup with 4 new adjustment factors + fix team stats

Bug fix:
- run_props_fetch now calls fetch_and_store_team_stats() so pace/defensive
  ratings are populated (previously fell back to league defaults for every team)

New adjustment factors (all multiplicative, individually capped):
1. Home/Away PPG split (±12% cap): uses player_game_log.is_home to compute
   location-specific avg; applied when n≥5 games at that location
2. H2H vs opponent (±20% cap): player's historical avg vs this specific team
   from player_game_log.opponent_abbr; applied when n≥3 matchups
3. Rest days extended: B2B ×0.92 (existing), 3+ days rest ×1.03 (new boost);
   rest_days field added to PlayerMatchupReport
4. Scoring trend (±5% cap): (l5/l10 - 1) × 0.30 transferred to projection;
   detects hot/cold streaks without overweighting short samples

New log line shows all factors:
  base=X pace=X def=X ha=X h2h=X rest=X trend=X → proj=X σ=X

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix miniupnpc API compatibility for version < 18

UPNP_GetValidIGD gained wanaddr/wanadrlen params in API version 18.
Add preprocessor guard so older versions (e.g. Ubuntu 24.04 ships v17)
use the 5-argument form.

feat: Engineering and Marketing sections in team report

Add two new sections below Business Metrics in the team report view,
backed by mock data shaped exactly like the real synced data.

Engineering (/teams/{id}/activity):
- Summary line: commit count, merged PRs, open issues
- Recent commits table: SHA, author, message, repo
- Open issues table: number, project board status (colour-coded), title, assignee
- Merged PRs table: number, title, author, merge date

Marketing (/teams/{id}/marketing):
- Per-campaign header: title, status (coloured), date range
- Per-task rows: bullet (○/●/✓ by status), title, status, assignee

Both endpoints return hardcoded mock data for now; the response shapes
mirror MarketingCampaign and the GitHub issue/PR/commit structs already
synced so wiring in real persistence is a straight swap.

commit.message:
fix: add fsync before rename in atomicWriteFile and atomicWriteFileAsync

Without fsync, a crash between write and rename could leave the temp
file with incomplete data. Use fd-level open/write/fsync/close pattern
consistent with the existing safeAppendFile implementation.

commit.message:
Fix people dedup: strip credentials and parentheticals before name key

Previous: "Col. John Lesinski, USMC (ret.)" → "john lesinski, usmc (ret.)"
          "JOHN LESINSKI" → "john lesinski"  (no match → duplicate)

Now: both normalize to "john lesinski" → deduped correctly.

Changes to _name_key():
- Strip everything after first comma (RN-BC, CHPCA, SCPO/USN, U.S. Army, etc.)
- Strip parentheticals like (ret.) or (USN)
- Expanded honorific/rank list (Capt, Maj, Adm, Cmdr, Cpl, Pvt)

arch: user decides on skill trust — system informs, never blocks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Strengthen coverage penalty to reduce pairing bias (#82)

Removed sqrt from coverage term: 1/√(1+n) → 1/(1+n).
This makes well-observed entries less likely to dominate pair selection.
Early testing shows this reduces the top entry's appearance rate from 28.7% to 25.2% against uniform baseline of 8.7%.

fix: Add auth redirect to middleware to prevent layout from overriding response

Layout redirect() creates a new HTTP response discarding all middleware
headers. By redirecting unauthenticated users in middleware, the layout
never runs and query params are preserved.

commit.message:
arch: setup doc — terminal in, seed, boot, close

System requires terminal access only during setup. After boot,
terminal is closed. Attack surface collapses to kernel ingress
and Redis, both behind mTLS. Prerequisites: kernel, graph DB,
Redis, model. All on brain for v1.
